description: Investigate whether wp-reconcile-evidence-verification (task adfbe699, drained earlier in this session) actually shipped its fix, or was itself false-positive-completed by the bug it targets. Deliverable is a short report at docs/analysis/reconcile-fix-shipped-verification.md with a verdict + evidence. NO code changes — if fix didn't ship, the report names a follow-up workplan.

tasks.title: Read docs/workplans/wp-reconcile-evidence-verification.json and list every file it promised to change. For each, run `git log --oneline -20 -- <file>` and assert at least one commit since 2026-04-14 touched the claimed code path. Record findings in docs/analysis/reconcile-fix-shipped-verification.md under ## File-Level Evidence with columns: promised_file, commits_since_enqueue, match_verdict (SHIPPED | MISSING | INCONCLUSIVE).

Pick a workplan from recent sched queue history that failed with 'no git evidence'. Manually invoke `hex plan execute <path>` and capture: (a) stdout/stderr, (b) number of tasks the planner thought were 'done' before execution, (c) whether any cargo/git/fs mutation actually happened. Record under ## Runtime Reproduction with exact commands + outputs.

Run `hex plan reconcile --dry-run --all` on this repo. For each workplan it claims is 'done', verify by cross-checking git log for commits touching the workplan's declared files[]. Record any workplans that reconcile claims are done but git log shows no matching commits as FALSE POSITIVES under ## False-Positive Reconciliations.

Write a ## Verdict section with one of: SHIPPED (fix landed and works), PARTIALLY SHIPPED (some files changed but bug still reproduces), NOT SHIPPED (workplan was false-positive-reconciled, no code changed). If NOT SHIPPED or PARTIALLY SHIPPED, the verdict section names a single follow-up workplan path (docs/workplans/wp-reconcile-evidence-verification-v2.json) that should be authored. Do NOT author that workplan in this task — name it only.
